title: Needs Assessment Summary
description: A structured memo to document and communicate your Analysis findings to stakeholders.
content:
NEEDS ASSESSMENT SUMMARY
========================
Project: [Project Name]
Date: [Date]
Prepared by: [Your Name]

BUSINESS PROBLEM
----------------
[Describe the organizational problem or opportunity. What is happening that shouldn't be, or not happening that should be?]

PERFORMANCE GAP
---------------
Current State: [What are people doing or not doing today?]
Desired State: [What should people be doing after training?]
Gap: [Describe the measurable difference]

ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS
--------------------
Is this a knowledge/skill issue? [Yes / No / Partial]
Explanation: [Describe root cause findings from interviews or observations]

AUDIENCE PROFILE
-----------------
Who: [Role(s) and number of learners]
Experience Level: [Beginner / Intermediate / Advanced]
Tech Access: [Device types, LMS access, bandwidth considerations]
Prior Knowledge: [What relevant knowledge do they already have?]

RECOMMENDED SOLUTION
---------------------
[Training / Job Aid / Process Change / Other — explain why]

PROJECT CONSTRAINTS
--------------------
Timeline: [Required completion date]
Budget: [Approximate range or limit]
Technology: [LMS, authoring tools, delivery format]
SME Availability: [Name, role, weekly hours available]

SUCCESS CRITERIA
-----------------
[How will we know this worked? What metrics will we look at and when?]

NEXT STEPS
----------
[ ] Schedule design kickoff meeting
[ ] Confirm SME availability and meeting cadence
[ ] Draft learning objectives for stakeholder review

Approved by: _________________________  Date: ___________

title: Learner Profile Template
description: Document your target audience in detail before designing anything.
content:
LEARNER PROFILE
===============
Project: [Project Name]
Date: [Date]

AUDIENCE OVERVIEW
------------------
Role(s): [Job title(s) of target learners]
Department(s): [Department or business unit]
Total Number of Learners: [Approximate headcount]
Geography: [Location(s) — remote, on-site, global]

KNOWLEDGE & EXPERIENCE
-----------------------
Time in Role (average): [e.g., 2–5 years]
Prior Training on This Topic: [Yes / No / Some]
Self-Assessed Proficiency: [Novice / Intermediate / Expert]
Key Knowledge Gaps: [List what they don't know that they need to]

ATTITUDES & MOTIVATION
-----------------------
Attitude Toward This Topic: [Resistant / Neutral / Motivated]
Perceived Relevance: [Do they see why this matters? Explain.]
Motivation Drivers: [What do they care about? Career growth, compliance, efficiency?]

LEARNING CONTEXT
-----------------
Where Will They Learn: [At desk / mobile / classroom / mix]
Time Available for Training: [e.g., 30-min max modules, full-day workshop]
Device Access: [Desktop / Laptop / Mobile / Tablet]
LMS or Platform: [Name of system]
Connectivity: [Reliable / Variable / Offline required]

ACCESSIBILITY CONSIDERATIONS
-----------------------------
[List any known accessibility needs or language/literacy considerations]

LEARNER INSIGHTS (from interviews/surveys)
-------------------------------------------
"[Direct quote from learner interview]" — [Role]
"[Direct quote from learner interview]" — [Role]

IMPLICATIONS FOR DESIGN
------------------------
[Based on this profile, what instructional decisions should we make?]

title: Learning Objectives Bank
description: A structured table to draft, review, and map your learning objectives.
content:
LEARNING OBJECTIVES BANK
=========================
Project: [Project Name]
Date: [Date]
Bloom's Level Key: 1=Remember | 2=Understand | 3=Apply | 4=Analyze | 5=Evaluate | 6=Create

TERMINAL OBJECTIVE(S)
----------------------
TO-1: Given [condition], [audience] will be able to [behavior] to [degree/standard].

TO-2: Given [condition], [audience] will be able to [behavior] to [degree/standard].

ENABLING OBJECTIVES
--------------------
[Maps to TO-1]
EO-1.1 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]
EO-1.2 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]
EO-1.3 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]

[Maps to TO-2]
EO-2.1 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]
EO-2.2 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]
EO-2.3 | Bloom's Level: [ ] | Objective: [Enabling objective]

OBJECTIVE QUALITY CHECKLIST
----------------------------
[ ] Each objective uses an observable, measurable verb
[ ] Each objective includes Audience, Behavior, Condition, and Degree
[ ] Bloom's level matches the actual job task complexity
[ ] Objectives are achievable within the course scope
[ ] SME has reviewed and approved all objectives

SME Sign-off: _________________________  Date: ___________
ID Sign-off:  _________________________  Date: ___________

title: Design Document (Blueprint)
description: The core contract between you and your stakeholders before a single asset is built.
content:
DESIGN DOCUMENT
===============
Project: [Project Name]
Version: 1.0
Date: [Date]
Instructional Designer: [Your Name]

PROJECT OVERVIEW
-----------------
Business Problem: [One sentence summary]
Target Audience: [Role, experience level, headcount]
Delivery Format: [e-learning / ILT / VILT / blended / job aid]
Estimated Seat Time: [e.g., 45 minutes]
Platform / LMS: [Platform name]

TERMINAL LEARNING OBJECTIVE
-----------------------------
[Full ABCD objective]

COURSE OUTLINE
--------------
Module 1: [Title]
  - Objective(s): EO-1.1, EO-1.2
  - Content: [Brief description]
  - Instructional Strategy: [e.g., scenario-based, lecture + practice]
  - Assessment: [e.g., 3-question knowledge check]
  - Estimated Time: [x min]

Module 2: [Title]
  - Objective(s): EO-2.1, EO-2.2
  - Content: [Brief description]
  - Instructional Strategy: [e.g., case study, demonstration]
  - Assessment: [e.g., branching scenario]
  - Estimated Time: [x min]

ASSESSMENT STRATEGY
--------------------
Pre-Assessment: [Yes / No] — Purpose: [adaptive skip / baseline data]
Knowledge Checks: [Frequency, format]
Final Assessment: [Format, number of items, passing score]
Performance Task: [If applicable — description]

MEDIA PLAN
-----------
Visuals: [Custom graphics / stock / screenshots]
Audio: [Narration / No narration / Music]
Video: [Talking head / screen capture / animation / none]
Interactivity: [Drag-and-drop / scenarios / simulations / none]

REVIEW AND REVISION PLAN
-------------------------
Alpha Review (internal ID): [Date]
Beta Review (SME): [Date]
Pilot: [Date, audience size]
Full Launch: [Date]

Approved by: _________________________  Date: ___________

title: Storyboard Template
description: A slide-by-slide blueprint for e-learning development. Complete before opening your authoring tool.
content:
STORYBOARD
==========
Project: [Project Name]
Module: [Module Number and Title]
Objective(s): [Objective codes this module addresses]
Version: 1.0 | Date: [Date]

---
SLIDE 001 | Type: Title
---
Visual: [Describe layout and any graphics]
On-Screen Text:
  Title: [Module Title]
  Subtitle: [Optional tagline or duration]
Narration/Audio: [Script or 'No narration']
Animation/Interaction: [Describe if any]
ID Notes: [Any notes for developer]

---
SLIDE 002 | Type: Objective
---
Visual: [e.g., Clean layout with bullet list]
On-Screen Text:
  By the end of this module, you will be able to:
  • [Enabling objective 1]
  • [Enabling objective 2]
Narration/Audio: [Script]
Animation/Interaction: [e.g., None / fade-in bullets]
ID Notes:

---
SLIDE 003 | Type: Content
---
Visual: [Describe graphic, diagram, or image]
On-Screen Text:
  [Headline]
  [Body text — keep to 2–3 key points max]
Narration/Audio: [Script]
Animation/Interaction: [Describe]
ID Notes:

---
SLIDE 004 | Type: Knowledge Check
---
Visual: [Question layout]
Question: [Question text]
A. [Option A]
B. [Option B]
C. [Option C]
D. [Option D]
Correct Answer: [Letter]
Feedback (Correct): [Reinforcement text]
Feedback (Incorrect): [Corrective explanation]
Narration/Audio: [If any]
ID Notes:

[Repeat slide blocks as needed]

title: Launch Communication Email
description: A ready-to-send email template for notifying learners that training is live.
content:
SUBJECT: New Training Available: [Course Title]

Hi [First Name / Team],

I'm pleased to let you know that [Course Title] is now available for you to complete.

WHY THIS TRAINING MATTERS
--------------------------
[Explain in 2–3 plain-language sentences why this training exists and what problem it solves. Connect it to something learners care about — their job, their team, a business goal.]

WHAT YOU'LL LEARN
------------------
By completing this training, you will be able to:
• [Key skill or outcome 1]
• [Key skill or outcome 2]
• [Key skill or outcome 3]

THE DETAILS
-----------
Format: [e-learning / live session / workshop]
Estimated Time: [X minutes]
Deadline: [Date, or 'Complete at your own pace']
Access: [Link to LMS or calendar invite]

HOW TO GET STARTED
-------------------
1. Click here: [LMS link]
2. Log in with your [company] credentials
3. Find [Course Title] in [location in LMS]

NEED HELP?
----------
If you have trouble accessing the training, contact [support email or name].
For questions about the content, reach out to [SME or manager name].

Thank you for your time and commitment to [learning goal].

[Your Name]
[Title]
[Department]

title: Manager Briefing One-Pager
description: Equip managers to support and reinforce training before and after launch.
content:
MANAGER BRIEFING: [Course Title]
=================================
Prepared for: [Manager Name / Management Team]
Date: [Date]

WHAT'S HAPPENING
-----------------
[Course Title] is launching on [date] for all [role/team name].
Estimated time: [X minutes] | Deadline: [Date]
Access: [LMS link or location]

WHY THIS MATTERS
-----------------
[Explain the business problem this training addresses. Be specific — what will change for the team as a result?]

YOUR ROLE AS A MANAGER
-----------------------
BEFORE the training:
[ ] Let your team know this is coming and why it's important
[ ] Block time on their calendars if needed
[ ] Set the expectation that completion by [deadline] is required/encouraged

DURING the training:
[ ] Be available to answer questions about how the content applies to your team's work

AFTER the training:
[ ] Have a brief team conversation about key takeaways
[ ] Connect training concepts to real on-the-job situations
[ ] Recognize and reinforce when team members apply what they learned

FAQ FOR MANAGERS
-----------------
Q: What if someone can't complete by the deadline?
A: [Instructions for extensions or exceptions]

Q: Who do I contact with questions about the content?
A: [SME name and contact]

Q: How will I know who on my team has completed it?
A: [LMS reporting instructions]

CONTACT
--------
For questions about this training initiative, contact:
[Instructional Designer Name] | [Email]

title: Level 1 Reaction Survey
description: A concise post-training survey to collect learner reaction data.
content:
POST-TRAINING SURVEY: [Course Title]
=====================================
Thank you for completing [Course Title]. This short survey (3–5 minutes) helps us improve future training.
All responses are anonymous.

SECTION 1: RELEVANCE
---------------------
1. The content of this training was relevant to my job.
   [ ] Strongly Agree  [ ] Agree  [ ] Neutral  [ ] Disagree  [ ] Strongly Disagree

2. The training addressed a real challenge I face in my work.
   [ ] Strongly Agree  [ ] Agree  [ ] Neutral  [ ] Disagree  [ ] Strongly Disagree

SECTION 2: QUALITY
-------------------
3. The training was well-organized and easy to follow.
   [ ] Strongly Agree  [ ] Agree  [ ] Neutral  [ ] Disagree  [ ] Strongly Disagree

4. The activities and examples helped me understand the content.
   [ ] Strongly Agree  [ ] Agree  [ ] Neutral  [ ] Disagree  [ ] Strongly Disagree

5. The length of the training was appropriate for the content.
   [ ] Too Short  [ ] About Right  [ ] Too Long

SECTION 3: INTENT TO APPLY
----------------------------
6. I plan to apply what I learned in this training to my work.
   [ ] Definitely  [ ] Probably  [ ] Unsure  [ ] Probably Not  [ ] Definitely Not

7. What is the most useful thing you will take away from this training?
   [Open text field]

8. What would make this training more effective?
   [Open text field]

OVERALL RATING
--------------
9. Overall, I would rate this training:
   [ ] Excellent  [ ] Good  [ ] Fair  [ ] Poor

Thank you for your feedback!
